notes: Packaged probability-bounds profiles are limited to single-driver perturbations with other drivers held fixed at the caller's base scenario.
These profiles publish cumulative probability bounds for selected support points and do not represent a joint population distribution.
Each packaged profile carries a curated driver family, product family, dependency cluster, fixed axes, and handling semantics.
Tier C probability-bounds outputs remain screening summaries and must not be interpreted as validated exposure distributions.

profiles.label: Adult leave-on hand cream use amount profile
profiles.description: Single-driver probability bounds for use amount per event in adult leave-on hand cream screening.

supportPoints.note: Lower screening support point for reduced use amount.
Reference support point aligned to the baseline screening example.
Upper screening support point for heavier product use.

limitations: This profile varies use amount only and holds frequency, concentration, and transfer semantics fixed.
The profile does not imply a validated consumer-use distribution.

profiles.label: Adult leave-on hand cream concentration profile
profiles.description: Single-driver probability bounds for concentration fraction in adult leave-on hand cream screening.

supportPoints.note: Lower screening support point for reduced formulation strength.
Reference support point aligned to the baseline screening example.
Upper screening support point for heavier formulation strength.

limitations: This profile varies concentration only and holds amount, frequency, and transfer semantics fixed.
The profile does not imply a validated formulation market distribution.

profiles.label: Adult trigger spray room volume profile
profiles.description: Single-driver probability bounds for room volume in trigger-spray inhalation screening.

supportPoints.note: Compact-room support point for higher concentration screening.
Reference room support point aligned to the baseline screening example.
Large-room support point for lower concentration screening.

limitations: This profile varies room volume only and does not resolve near-field plume behavior.
Ventilation, duration, and release mechanics remain fixed at the base scenario.

profiles.label: Child direct-oral liquid frequency profile
profiles.description: Single-driver probability bounds for use frequency in child direct-oral liquid screening.

supportPoints.note: Lower screening support point for reduced direct-oral frequency.
Reference support point aligned to the baseline direct-oral example.
Upper screening support point for heavier direct-oral frequency.

limitations: This profile varies dosing frequency only and keeps amount, strength, and ingestion completeness fixed.
The profile does not imply a validated pediatric regimen distribution.
